data.body:
[PASS 1 / E4 — Assumption/premise verification] PASS, no findings. Probed every codebase assertion in the plan:
- "blocking change to the in-use `claim` (open->in_progress) path" — VERIFIED: rebar.toml:14-21 (`require_plan_review_for_claim = true`, "claiming a work ticket (open->in_progress) requires a fresh, certified plan-review attestation ... BLOCK the claim").
- "`--force` per-claim is not a system-level kill switch" — VERIFIED: rebar.toml:16-17 ("`--force` bypasses with an audit comment"); this is per-invocation, confirming the plan's premise that no project-level off-switch exists today (rebar.toml:20 "Turning this off is the rollback" is a comment-only assertion, not a tested config path — consistent with the plan's gap).
- "REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar scheme" / reducer-ignored — VERIFIED: src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44 (`_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES = frozenset({"SYNC", "PRECONDITIONS", "REVIEW_RESULT"})`) and tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:16,156-174.
- "older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events — cross-check with the TAG_DELTA/event-rollout note" — VERIFIED: src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:31-37 (v3 forward-compat: "an older (v2) clone preserves-and-ignores `TAG_DELTA`") and tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py:192-206; docs/event-schema.md:71,93.
All four assertions are grounded in implementation/test files, not training knowledge or contract-doc-only claims. No unverifiable assertion remains; nothing to fail-close on.
